Interesting - LEFT changed the board but the player position stayed at [34, 16]. So the board changed but the player didn't move. Let me check what actually changed - maybe the step counter or some other HUD element changed.
Let me compare the before and after frames to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after frames to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Check segmentation differences
before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

print("Before nodes:")
for n in before_seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, hash={n['hash'][:8]}")

print("\nAfter nodes:")
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, hash={n['hash'][:8]}")

# Check if any node boundaries changed
print("\nBoundary changes:")
for i, (bn, an) in enumerate(zip(before_seg['nodes'], after_seg['nodes'])):
    if bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
        print(f"  Node {i}: {bn['boundary']} -> {an['boundary']}")
    if bn['pixels'] != an['pixels']:
        print(f"  Node {i} pixels: {bn['pixels']} -> {an['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

else:
